title: PRECEPT Nurses Act
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on Ways and Means.
plain_summary: The PRECEPT Nurses Act creates a tax credit to help train more nurses. Experienced nurses can get $2,000 back on their taxes if they spend at least 200 hours teaching nursing students or new nurses in areas that don't have enough healthcare workers. This tax credit will be available from 2026 through 2032. The government will keep track of how well this program works and report to Congress about it each year.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Ways and Means.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Providing Real-world Education and Clinical Experience by Precepting Tomorrow's Nurses Act or the PRECEPT Nurses Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Credit for Nurse Preceptors
sections.text: This section changes the tax code to create a new tax credit for nurse preceptors. A nurse preceptor is an experienced nurse who teaches and guides nursing students or new nurses. If someone qualifies as an eligible nurse preceptor, they can get a two thousand dollar tax credit each year. To qualify, a person must serve at least two hundred hours during the year as a nurse preceptor in an area that has been officially marked as having a shortage of healthcare workers. The Internal Revenue Service will publish a list of these shortage areas on their website each year. A nurse preceptor means a licensed registered nurse or healthcare provider who supervises and teaches nursing students or newly hired nurses. This teaching includes hands-on training, instruction, and mentoring in real nursing practice. A newly hired nurse means someone who has been working for less than six months. To get the tax credit, the nurse preceptor must get a written certification proving they completed at least two hundred hours of precepting work. This certification must come from either the school where they taught nursing students or from the healthcare facility where they work if they trained new nurses. A person can get multiple certifications from different places to reach the two hundred hour requirement. This tax credit will only be available for tax years starting before January first two thousand thirty three. The changes will begin for tax years starting after December thirty first two thousand twenty five. The Treasury Secretary must report to Congress each year from two thousand twenty six through two thousand thirty two about how many people are using this tax credit, how many hours they worked, where they are located, and other useful information. By June thirtieth two thousand thirty three, the Treasury Secretary must work with health agencies to evaluate how well this tax credit worked to increase the number of nurse preceptors in the United States and report this to Congress.
